beats.narration: Echo height is set by area and distance. Double a flat bottom hole's diameter in the far field: twelve decibels louder.

payload.title: ECHO ARITHMETIC
payload.sub: Area and distance set the height of every echo.

beats.narration: Distance taxes every echo. Per doubling, a back wall drops about six decibels, a side drilled hole nine, a small disc twelve.

payload.heading: How echoes fall in the far field

beats.narration: Deep flaws read low. Peak the same size hole at several depths and connect the marks: a DAC, judging each echo at its own range.

payload.heading: The fix: distance amplitude correction
payload.note: JUDGE EACH ECHO AT ITS OWN RANGE

beats.narration: No method sees everything. Ultrasound owns planar flaws and depth, radiography volume and shape, mag particle and penetrant surface connection. Corroborate, never average.

chips.t: MT / PT: open to the surface?

beats.narration: So the rule: amplitude means nothing until distance is corrected away. Correct first, then judge, and let the disposition follow the evidence.

payload.sub: The disposition belongs to the evidence — not one screen.
